summary: High‑quality soundboard capture of a tight Greek ’84 closer, highlighted by a blazing Dancing > Bird Song opener and the epic China/Rider/Playing in the Band/Uncle John’s sequence.
review: The recording delivers a clear, well‑balanced soundboard mix that still suffers from occasional instrument‑level quirks (Garcia buried, Brent’s keys swinging high or low). Musically the show is a standout – Jerry is “off the charts,” the opener Dancing > Bird Song sets an electric tone, and the China/Rider/Playing in the Band/Uncle John’s stretch ranks among the band’s best 30‑minute jams. Overall fans rave about both performance and fidelity.

ai_rating.rationale: Four detailed, high‑scoring reviews praise the musical performance (Jerry’s playing, standout sequences) and note good but slightly uneven recording balance; weighted average yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and lively soundboard capture; some reviewers note Garcia’s guitar low in the mix and Brent’s keyboards unusually high or low, but overall fidelity is praised.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight Greek closer with an energetic opener and a second set that maintains momentum; the sequence China/Rider/Playing in the Band/UJB is highlighted as a seamless, high‑energy centerpiece.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised – “off the charts,” “like vintage ’74,” possessed playing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as light on the mix (low bass) but still comes through well on speakers.
band_member_comments.Bob: Described as letting it all out on Throwing Stones, indicating strong rhythm guitar/vocal work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards are mentioned for being too high in most SBDs and low in this tape; performance is lauded when he “cuts loose.”
